response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned generalist engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js fundamentals, staff-level seniority, and a brief but directly relevant stint building MCP tooling and agentic-workflow infrastructure at re:cinq. However, the role demands 8+ years of production AI delivery, and his AI exposure is confined to roughly six months in 2025, with no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks. Python—the dominant language for the AI stack described—does not appear anywhere in his resume. The current career gap, combined with thin GenAI depth against explicit senior requirements, makes him a poor fit at this seniority level.
strengths: Hands-on experience building MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks—directly listed as a key technology in the JD
Genuine staff-level engineering background with a track record of cross-domain technical leadership, open-source platform ownership, and mentoring through workshops and talks
AWS CDK exposure and microservices experience at scale (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby) demonstrate cloud-infrastructure competence
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume; the JD lists it as the primary required language and it is the de-facto standard for the entire AI tooling ecosystem described
No demonstrated experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI
AI/GenAI work spans only ~6 months (re:cinq, April–October 2025) against a hard requirement of 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions; currently in an open-ended career gap
